title: Application State Machine, Commands, and Recovery · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved maze trace contains one plausible coordinate, wall, graph, search, event-loop, focus, render, persistence, package, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in explicit application transition table and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define idle, generating, ready, solving, paused, completed, cancelled, failed, and closing states with owned data.
Admit commands through an explicit current-state transition table with effects and rejection feedback.
Derive enabled controls, labels, focus targets, status, and progress from state rather than mutating them independently.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

For transition testing · fault isolation, solve one convenient seeded maze, omit revision, coordinate and wall invariants, topology oracle, frontier trace, changed algorithm or heuristic, callback ownership, keyboard and structured-text access, native Tk evidence, failure, recovery, and package transfer, then accept a screenshot as maze proof. This does not satisfy Transition Testing for explicit application transition table.

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Transition Testing
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-transition-testing-rn87bq" that uses case 3681 to verify explicit application transitions, command rejection, control projection, and recoverable failure states. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Transition Testing.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Transition Testing into observable performance evidence. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Transition Testing, keep Transition Testing connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-transition-testing-rn87bq
# Competency: maze-transition-testing.
# Case 3681: a corrupted-state recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def evidence_maze_transition_testing_rn87bq(allowed_transitions: int = 18, verified_transitions: int = 18, rejected_commands: int = 9, unsafe_transitions: int = 0, recoverable_failures: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_rn87bq = "rn87bq-qb78nr-3681"
    assert evidence_variant_rn87bq
    evidence_axes_rn87bq = (
        "gridrn87bq",
        "topology3681",
        "searchqb78nr",
        "eventrn87bq3681",
        "access3681rn87bq",
        "recoveryqb78nr3681",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_rn87bq)) == 6
    if allowed_transitions < 1 or verified_transitions < 0:
        return False, verified_transitions, "transition-count"
    if verified_transitions != allowed_transitions:
        return False, verified_transitions, "transition-coverage"
    if rejected_commands < 1 or unsafe_transitions > 0:
        return False, unsafe_transitions, "command-admission"
    if recoverable_failures < 1:
        return False, recoverable_failures, "recovery-path"
    assert verified_transitions == allowed_transitions
    return True, verified_transitions, "verified-state-machine"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Transition Testing structural check is only one gate. explicit application transition table must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Cover every allowed and rejected edge, repeated command, cancellation race, failure, and close transition.
